frame_label: Prenup / Alimony as Reverse Dowry / Women as Golddiggers
generated_comment: Honestly, every young man should think about legal protection before marriage now. Aajkal one-sided laws have made divorce a financial trap for men, and even 498A gets used as pressure tactic in many cases. If a woman is genuinely interested in building a life together, she should have no issue with a prenup and fair terms instead of treating marriage like long-term investment exit money.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women broadly as financially manipulative and casts legal protections as tools women use to exploit men. It encodes the 'prenup as protection from women,' 'one-sided laws,' and '498A misuse' manifestations in a concern-for-men's-rights register.
original_frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 36. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are commenting under your real name on a Facebook post where someone has shared an article about marital rape legislation. The comment section is divided. You are writing a detailed comment explaining why you think criminalising marital rape will destroy Indian marriages, why consent within marriage is a western concept that does not map onto Indian family structures, and what you think wives who use this law will use it for.
